title: Social Security: Improving Hiring Processes at State Disability Determination Services (SSAB Issue Brief, September 2025)
description: Social Security Advisory Board (SSAB) issue brief (September 2025) examining recurrent staffing pressures and the impact of episodic hiring freezes and hiring-approval timing at the state Disability Determination Services (DDS), with recommendations to inform SSA hiring policy. Background in the brief: DDSs determine medical eligibility for Disability Insurance (DI) and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) at the initial and reconsideration levels and conduct continuing disability reviews; they are entirely SSA-funded but state-managed and -staffed, and must comply with SSA policy including when a DDS may hire. The brief cites the National Council of Disability Determination Directors (NCDDD) on hiring-freeze harms and an SSA OIG report finding that, FY2019-FY2024, case processing times and queue size rose while experienced examiners dropped 11% and medical consultants dropped 13%; and SSA Office of the Chief Actuary analysis attributing a ~20% drop in DDS determinations to a 21% drop in fully trained examiners diverted to train new hires. Board members listed: Bob Joondeph (Chair), Nancy J. Altman, Jagadeesh Gokhale, Amy Shuart.
geographic_coverage: United States (federal — SSA, national state DDS)

source.title: Social Security: Improving Hiring Processes at State Disability Determination Services (Issue Brief)
